title: Easy Aerobic Z2
description: Foundation aerobic run. The majority of weekly volume should be here. Conversational pace, HR in Z2.

steps.description: 50 min Z2 — conversational pace, 65-75% LTHR. Nose breathing target.

title: Threshold Tempo 20 min
description: Sustained lactate threshold pace. Comfortably hard — you can speak a sentence, not a paragraph.

steps.description: 20 min easy warmup — build to Z3 by end
20 min @ threshold — 92-95% LTHR, race pace feel. Even effort.

title: Cruise Intervals 5×8 min
description: Threshold repeats with short rest. Accumulates threshold time while staying fresher than a solid tempo block.

steps.description: 5×8 min @ threshold (92-95% LTHR) — 90s jog rest between

title: VO2max 4×4 min
description: Norwegian-style VO2max intervals. Short enough to hold quality, long enough to drive adaptation. 3 min active recovery.

steps.description: 20 min easy — include 4×30s strides in final 5 min
4×4 min @ 100-103% LTHR — 3 min jog recovery. Hard but not all-out.

title: Track 400m Repeats 8×400
description: Classic track session for speed and lactate tolerance. Full recovery. Hit the same split every rep.

steps.description: 8×400m @ 5K race pace — 90s full standing rest. First 2 feel easy. Last 2 hard.

title: Track 800m Repeats 6×800
description: 800m repeats at 5K pace. Build lactate tolerance and running economy. Active recovery jogs.

steps.description: 6×800m @ 5K–10K pace — 2 min jog recovery. Negative split each rep.

title: Long Run Z2 90 min
description: Weekly long run cornerstone. Builds aerobic base, fat oxidation, durability. Easy throughout.

steps.description: 85 min Z2 — never above Z3. Walk hills if needed to keep HR honest.

title: Long Run with Race Pace Finish
description: 90 min long run with 20 min race pace finish. IM-specific — practice pacing off the bike on tired legs.

steps.description: 70 min Z2 — comfortable, building fatigue intentionally
20 min @ IM race pace — lift effort without blowing up. This is the adaptation.

title: Fartlek 30 min
description: Unstructured surges on feel. No watch targets — run by effort. Great for mental freshness.

steps.description: 6× surge 1 min hard / 2 min easy — go by feel, no prescribed pace

title: Hill Repeats 8×60s
description: Strength-building hill sprints. Develops run-specific power and glute engagement. Full walk-down recovery.

steps.description: 8×60s hard uphill — drive knees, pump arms. Walk back down full recovery.

title: Marathon Pace 3×3km
description: Sustained marathon/IM run pace repeats. Develops race-pace economy. Moderate effort — not a hard session.

steps.description: 3×3km @ marathon pace — 87-90% LTHR. 3 min easy jog between.

title: Olympic Tri Run Off-Bike
description: Run directly off the bike (brick). Starts with jelly legs — hold patience for first km, build to race pace.

steps.description: First 5 min — let legs come around, hold back
40 min @ Olympic race pace — 90-95% LTHR, push last 10 min

title: Recovery Jog 30 min
description: Active recovery. Flush the legs — never let HR climb above Z1. If it does, walk.

steps.description: 30 min very easy — walk breaks as needed. HR never above Z1.
